When a Server Action forwards or redirects a request, an attacker can cause the server to send that outbound request to a malicious host (Server-Side Request Forgery). This requires the attacker's request to control Host-associated headers. In some configurations, it's also possible to obtain internal values that weaken middleware/proxy authorization.

Applications that use Server Actions are affected when the incoming host header is not fixed to a trusted value. This typically occurs on custom servers, or on deployments not behind a proxy that pins the host. Managed hosting pins the host upstream and is not affected; `next start` and standalone output do the same from version 14.2 onward.

## Workarounds

If you cannot upgrade, ensure clients do not control the host header your application receives. Pin or validate `Host` and `X-Forwarded-Host` at your edge or proxy. On version 14.2.0 and later, you can additionally set the `__NEXT_PRIVATE_ORIGIN` environment variable to your deployment's real origin:

```bash
__NEXT_PRIVATE_ORIGIN=https://www.example.com node server.js", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-89xv-2m56-2m9x", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-22T23:09:48Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-89xv-2m56-2m9x", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-89xv-2m56-2m9x"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-64649"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-07-22T23:09:48Z", "references": ["https://github.com/vercel/next.js/security/advisories/GHSA-89xv-2m56-2m9x", "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/commit/b51206321854193208c0805ba42acc49287f942b", "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/commit/e3e5666ccead3a15162793d697af5e48b7cc0498", "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/releases/tag/v15.5.21", "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/releases/tag/v16.2.11", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-89xv-2m56-2m9x"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/vercel/next.js/security-advisories/GHSA-89xv-2m56-2m9x", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/vercel/next.js", "summary": "Next.js: Server-Side Request Forgery in Server Actions on custom servers", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-22T23:09:50Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-89xv-2m56-2m9x", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "15.5.21", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "next"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 14.1.1, < 15.5.21"}, {"first_patched_version": "16.2.11", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "next"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 16.0.0, < 16.2.11"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Next.js: Server-Side Request Forgery in Server Actions on custom servers
